audit_text: RACE CONDITION — bulk-close broadcast (sessions_bulk.py:333-340). After bulk tx commits, code re-fetches rows to build broadcast. Concurrent delete in that window → row=None → close not broadcast to other tabs. Fix: capture rows inside the tx before commit, or use data returned by _bulk_close() directly.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/sessions.md §"Bulk operations contract" → "Frontend observable behavior" → Close ("on any ok=true entries, pushes an undo toast … partial failures surface as an inline error banner above the session list") implicitly requires every ok=true entry to broadcast an upsert so other tabs refresh.
verdict_evidence: _bulk_close at L155-190 commits the transaction at L189 and returns BulkResultItems. The route then iterates results at L332-340 and, for each ok result, executes 'row = await sessions_db.get(db, r.session_id)' (L338) and only broadcasts when 'row is not None' (L339-340). Between the commit and the per-id re-fetch a concurrent DELETE from another tab/op can drop the row, leaving row=None and silently swallowing the broadcast — the audit's race window is exactly the gap between L189 and L338. git log -S on the characteristic re-fetch line resolves to the introducing commit bf3bf102; no resolving commit exists in HEAD.
acceptance_criteria: _bulk_close returns enough data (e.g. list[Session] of the rows it just closed, or list[(session_id, closed_at, updated_at, ...full row state...)] tuples) for the route to construct SessionOut payloads without a post-commit re-fetch.
The route's BULK_OP_CLOSE branch publishes one upsert per ok result using the data returned by _bulk_close(); no call to sessions_db.get(db, r.session_id) survives in the broadcast path.

A regression test simulates the race by deleting a session row between _bulk_close()'s commit and the broadcast (e.g. monkeypatch sessions_db.get to delete-then-return-None) and asserts the broadcaster still receives publish_upsert for that session id.
executor_brief: Race in POST /api/sessions/bulk's close branch: _bulk_close commits, then the route re-fetches each closed row to broadcast — a concurrent delete in that window kills the broadcast. Refactor _bulk_close to return the closed Session rows (or pre-built SessionOut payloads) before the outer commit and have the route iterate that in-memory list to publish upserts. Add a test that injects a delete during the race window and asserts publish_upsert still fires.

audit_text: TAG CARDINALITY NOT ENFORCED AT PATCH (sessions.py:~509). _validate_tag_cardinality() runs at create but not at PATCH /api/sessions/{id}. Incremental single-attach edits can land a session in a 2-project-tag state. Add same validation call in patch path. Same bug independently surfaced by audit #5 — high confidence.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/sessions.md §"Inline rename" + behavior of PATCH /api/sessions/{id} (the SessionUpdate body carries tag_ids); cardinality rule is documented in src/bearings/web/routes/sessions.py:165-176 (≤1 project / ≤1 severity per session, enforced at the API boundary).
code_paths_checked: src/bearings/web/routes/sessions.py:435-516 (patch_session — bulk-replaces tag set at L510 via tags_db.set_for_session, no cardinality check anywhere in the handler)
verdict_evidence: create_session calls _validate_tag_cardinality(db, tag_ids) at L371 before persisting. patch_session accepts payload.tag_ids (validated for existence at L456-473) and writes via tags_db.set_for_session at L510 — the bulk-replace path explicitly named in _validate_tag_cardinality's docstring as one that DOES need validation. There is no _validate_tag_cardinality call in patch_session, so a PATCH with two project-class tag_ids succeeds and the session row lands in a 2-project state. The single-attach carve-out called out in the docstring (PUT /api/sessions/{sid}/tags/{tid}) does not apply here — PATCH is bulk-replace. Confirmed by audit #5 independently. No commit in HEAD touches this gap.
suggested_files_to_change: tests/web/routes/test_sessions_patch.py (or whichever test module covers PATCH /api/sessions/{id})
acceptance_criteria: patch_session calls await _validate_tag_cardinality(db, new_tag_ids) after the existence check and before tags_db.set_for_session writes.
PATCH /api/sessions/{id} with two project-class tag_ids returns 422 with a detail string mentioning '≤1 project tag allowed'.
PATCH /api/sessions/{id} with two severity-class tag_ids returns 422 with '≤1 severity tag allowed'.
PATCH /api/sessions/{id} with one project + one severity + N other-class tag_ids still succeeds (200).
executor_brief: PATCH /api/sessions/{id} replaces the tag set wholesale via tags_db.set_for_session but skips the ≤1-project / ≤1-severity check that create_session runs through _validate_tag_cardinality. Add the same validator call in patch_session before the write, and add unit tests for the two violation paths plus a passing mixed-class path. The validator already exists; this is a one-line plus tests.

audit_text: BULK EXPORT NULL FILTERING MISSING IN FRONTEND (sessionsBulk.ts:148-150). Behavior doc says callers filter nulls before triggering download. TS client returns resp.blob() raw. Either filter client-side or move to server and update doc.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/sessions.md §"Bulk operations contract" → "Response — export op" (lines 302-313): "`null` slots represent session IDs that were not found. The frontend filters them out before triggering the download."
code_paths_checked: frontend/src/lib/components/sidebar/SessionList.svelte:456-466 (handleMultiExport: URL.createObjectURL on raw blob; no filtering between fetch and download)
src/bearings/web/routes/sessions_bulk.py:301-311 (server bundles SessionExport|None into BulkExportOut.sessions and json.dumps it as-is)
verdict_evidence: Server-side, the export op assembles 'session_exports: list[SessionExport | None]' and dumps it directly (sessions_bulk.py:302-311) — null slots reach the wire. Client-side, bulkExportSessions returns 'await resp.blob()' raw (sessionsBulk.ts:150) and handleMultiExport (SessionList.svelte:459-465) hands that blob straight to <a download> — no JSON parse, no .filter(Boolean), no re-serialization. The behavior doc explicitly delegates this filtering to the frontend; no implementation fulfils that contract. git log on sessionsBulk.ts shows only the introducing commit bf3bf102.
acceptance_criteria: bulkExportSessions parses the server response as JSON, filters null entries from sessions[], and returns a Blob containing the filtered JSON (text/json or application/json) so the downloaded file contains no null slots.
A unit test posts a server response with mixed SessionExport + null entries and asserts the returned Blob's text contains only the SessionExport entries.
handleMultiExport in SessionList.svelte still triggers a download with the same filename pattern bearings-export-YYYY-MM-DD.json — no caller-side change required.

If the alternative (server-side filter) is taken instead, the docs/behavior/sessions.md §"Response — export op" prose is updated to say nulls are stripped server-side, and the server-side filter is added in src/bearings/web/routes/sessions_bulk.py:301-311.
executor_brief: Bulk export of multi-selection sends the server's null-padded array straight to the user's download — the spec says the frontend filters those nulls. Update bulkExportSessions to JSON-parse the response, drop nulls from sessions[], and return a Blob of the filtered JSON. Add a vitest case alongside the existing 'bulkExportSessions posts to /api/sessions/bulk once' test in SessionList.bulk.test.ts that feeds a mixed null+SessionExport response and asserts the returned Blob is null-free.

audit_text: CHECKLIST SESSIONS CAN BE CLOSED (sessions.py:537-551). No kind guard on POST /api/sessions/{id}/close. Results in kind=checklist + closed_at IS NOT NULL. Prompt endpoint rejects 409 anyway so nothing breaks, but it is a reachable inconsistent state.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/sessions.md §"Bulk operations contract" plus docs/behavior/prompt-endpoint.md §"Failure responses" line 82 (only chat / checklist sessions are runnable; closed sessions return 409); docs/behavior/checklists.md treats checklist sessions as long-lived and not subject to the chat close/reopen lifecycle.
code_paths_checked: src/bearings/web/routes/sessions.py:67-73 (KNOWN_SESSION_KINDS imported; close handler does not consult it)
verdict_evidence: close_session at L537-551 fetches the closed row from sessions_db.close and returns it without examining row.kind. KNOWN_SESSION_KINDS is imported at L67-73 but not consulted. A POST /api/sessions/{id}/close on a kind='checklist' row succeeds and writes closed_at — the inconsistent state the audit names. The prompt endpoint's 409 gate (per prompt-endpoint.md §Failure responses) prevents user-visible breakage downstream, but the row state itself is still wrong. No commit in HEAD adds the kind guard.
suggested_files_to_change: src/bearings/config/constants.py (only if introducing a new CLOSEABLE_SESSION_KINDS constant; otherwise no change needed)
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/sessions/{id}/close on a kind='checklist' session returns 422 with a detail string explaining checklist sessions cannot be closed (or another non-2xx code matching the prompt-endpoint convention).
POST /api/sessions/{id}/close on a kind='chat' session continues to return 200 with a closed_at timestamp set.
The kind allow-list lives in a named constant (CLOSEABLE_SESSION_KINDS = frozenset({'chat'}) or equivalent) in bearings.config.constants — no inline literal in the route body.
A regression test exercises the chat-success and checklist-rejection paths.
executor_brief: POST /api/sessions/{id}/close has no kind guard, so checklist sessions can land in (kind='checklist', closed_at IS NOT NULL) — a reachable inconsistent state even though the prompt endpoint's 409 gate masks the downstream effect. Add a kind check in close_session that returns 422 for non-chat kinds, backed by a CLOSEABLE_SESSION_KINDS constant in bearings.config.constants. Add tests for both the chat-success and checklist-rejection paths.
